paper.abstract_en: Cosmologically coupled black holes (CCBHs), whose masses evolve in response to the cosmological expansion, have recently attracted significant theoretical and observational interest. Existing studies have treated CCBHs as purely classical objects, neglecting the effect of Hawking radiation (HR), which competes with the cosmological coupling (CC) mechanism. We take a first step towards studying evaporating CCBHs, adopting a quasi-adiabatic approximation in which the HR rate is evaluated at the instantaneous CCBH mass, and modeling the CC mechanism through the phenomenological scaling of the mass with the scale factor, $M \propto a^k$. We show that, depending on the coupling strength $k$, even late-time CC activation can significantly delay Hawking evaporation, or lead to asymptotic CC-dominated mass growth, with important implications. We set limits on the abundance of primordial CCBHs from $γ$-ray observations, finding limits which are weaker than their uncoupled counterparts, as CCBHs are kept farther from the endpoint of evaporation for a longer time. Unlike standard primordial black holes, the CCBH formation and present-day masses no longer approximately coincide, even for formation masses $M_{\text{form}} \gtrsim 10^{15}\,{\text{g}}$. Therefore, the same population of primordial CCBHs may be subject to evaporation limits through its past emission history, as well as to other limits (such as microlensing) through its present-day mass.

decision.selection_reason_zh: 该论文研究了宇宙学耦合黑洞的蒸发，结合霍金辐射与宇宙学耦合机制，提出了准绝热近似模型，对原初黑洞的观测限制有重要影响，主题契合黑洞热力学。
decision.title_zh: 蒸发的宇宙学耦合黑洞
decision.abstract_zh: 宇宙学耦合黑洞（CCBH），其质量响应宇宙膨胀而演化，最近引起了显著的理论和观测兴趣。现有研究将CCBH视为纯经典物体，忽略了霍金辐射（HR）的影响，霍金辐射与宇宙学耦合（CC）机制竞争。我们迈出了研究蒸发的CCBH的第一步，采用准绝热近似，其中HR速率在瞬时CCBH质量下评估，并通过质量随尺度因子的现象学缩放$M \propto a^k$来建模CC机制。我们表明，取决于耦合强度$k$，即使是晚期的CC激活也能显著延迟霍金蒸发，或导致渐近的CC主导的质量增长，具有重要含义。我们从$\gamma$射线观测中设定了原初CCBH丰度的限制，发现这些限制比其非耦合对应物更弱，因为CCBH在更长时间内保持远离蒸发终点。与标准原初黑洞不同，CCBH的形成质量和现今质量不再近似一致，即使对于形成质量$M_{\text{form}} \gtrsim 10^{15}\,{\text{g}}$也是如此。因此，同一原初CCBH群体可能通过其过去发射历史受到蒸发限制，以及通过其现今质量受到其他限制（如微引力透镜）。
